prompt:
Scene:
A square competition-poster composition set in the observation deck of a silent orbital palace, with midnight blue glass, distant planet glow, polished metal handrails, and restrained silver highlights. The mood is solemn and expensive rather than sci-fi loud, with generous negative space and a premium one-sheet balance that suggests official festival packaging. The frame should feel ready for invisible title and laurel placement without containing any readable text.

Subject:
Create a serious fandom-crossover poster centered on an original trio: a young empress in severe white tailoring, her disinherited brother in a dark ceremonial uniform, and an elderly navigator in a translucent visor. They should stand far enough apart to imply political fracture, but close enough to read as one family tragedy. The setting hints at a space-opera dynasty, yet the treatment must remain intimate and human, more arthouse succession drama than battle spectacle. Use window reflections, subtle costume insignia shapes, and careful eye lines to communicate hierarchy.

Important details:
The challenge is translating franchise-scale lore into an austere award-festival visual language. Keep the poster sparse, polished, and emotionally legible. Use precise facial lighting, controlled metallic textures, and soft cosmic color gradients without turning the image into concept-art overload. The characters should feel instantly iconic, but the packaging must read as a genuine competition-film key art image.

Use case:
Festival Poster Fandom Crossover single study 5/9, testing whether a space-opera royal family can be compressed into restrained competition-poster packaging.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, no spaceship battle, no laser effects, no crowded starfield wallpaper, no floating heads collage, no campy science-fiction camp, no superhero posing, no glossy franchise merchandise feel.
title: Orbit Royalty Competition
description: A restrained orbital-dynasty poster translated into serious competition-film packaging.

generation.runner_notes: Independent festival poster study translating a space-opera dynasty into prestige competition packaging.

en.title: Orbit Royalty Competition
en.description: A restrained orbital-dynasty poster translated into serious competition-film packaging.
